_meta.note: Only confirm relations with explicit external evidence. Delete curated high-sensitivity false positives and low-value Perplexity colleague_no_shared_org relations after bucket review.

decisions.evidenceNote: OpenAI launch post lists Ilya Sutskever, Greg Brockman, Andrej Karpathy, John Schulman, Wojciech Zaremba as founding members and names Sam Altman / Elon Musk as co-chairs/funders.
Computerworld reports DNNresearch was set up by Geoffrey Hinton and graduate students Alex Krizhevsky and Ilya Sutskever.
Anthropic-sponsored AWS re:Invent deck lists Dario Amodei as CEO & Co-founder and Chris Olah as Co-founder.
WIRED reports Thinking Machines is led by Mira Murati and lists John Schulman among her cofounders.
VentureBeat reports Richard Socher launched AIX Ventures and describes Socher and his cofounders, including Chris Manning, as joining together as a venture firm.
The arXiv record for Attention Is All You Need lists Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Jakob Uszkoreit, Aidan N. Gomez and Lukasz Kaiser as coauthors.
Stanford Report describes Fei-Fei Li and Chris Manning among HAI's original founders.
The ImageNet CVPR 2009 publication record lists Richard Socher and Li Fei-Fei as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks lists Ilya Sutskever, Oriol Vinyals and Quoc V. Le as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models lists Hyung Won Chung, Jason Wei and Quoc V. Le among the coauthors.
The arXiv record for Generative Adversarial Nets lists Ian Goodfellow and Yoshua Bengio as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate lists Dzmitry Bahdanau and Yoshua Bengio as coauthors.
The NeurIPS record for ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks lists Alex Krizhevsky and Ilya Sutskever as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models lists Jason Wei and Hyung Won Chung among the coauthors.
The arXiv record for Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems lists Lukasz Kaiser and John Schulman as coauthors.
ACM named Yoshua Bengio, Geoffrey Hinton and Yann LeCun as the 2018 A.M. Turing Award recipients.
OpenAI's launch post lists John Schulman as an early research engineer and Elon Musk as a co-chair/funder, supporting the early OpenAI relationship edge.
TechCrunch reports Daniel Gross met YC's Paul Graham shortly before Demo Day and Graham gave concrete product-direction advice; this supports the YC mentor/advisor relation.
The arXiv record for Deep reinforcement learning from human preferences lists Jan Leike, Shane Legg and Dario Amodei as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Grammar as a Foreign Language lists Oriol Vinyals, Lukasz Kaiser and Geoffrey Hinton as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Building high-level features using large scale unsupervised learning lists Quoc V. Le and Jeff Dean as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code lists Jan Leike, Lukasz Kaiser and Wojciech Zaremba as coauthors.
The Nature AlphaFold paper lists Oriol Vinyals and Demis Hassabis among the authors.
The arXiv record for Learning Language Games through Interaction lists Percy Liang and Christopher D. Manning as coauthors.
The JMLR paper Learning Algorithms for the Classification Restricted Boltzmann Machine lists Hugo Larochelle and Yoshua Bengio as authors.
The referenced Concrete Problems in AI Safety author list includes Chris Olah but not Geoffrey Hinton; the concrete coauthor claim is unsupported.

These remaining project/forum/company-level descriptions do not provide a source-backed personal collaborator edge; the product relation surface should not show company partnerships, public-dialogue appearances or common-signature claims as collaborator relations.
The arXiv record for Architectural Complexity Measures of Recurrent Neural Networks lists Ruslan Salakhutdinov and Yoshua Bengio as coauthors.
The arXiv record for Google's Neural Machine Translation System lists Quoc V. Le and Lukasz Kaiser as coauthors.

Final collaborator bucket review found no direct source-backed paper/project evidence for these broad publication or education-collaboration claims; leaving them visible would overstate weak or unsupported edges.

Final colleague bucket review found no date-proven role overlap for these same-organization colleague claims; several current PersonRole timelines explicitly start after the other person left, and the remaining rows are broad organization claims without shared dated employment evidence.
Final colleague bucket review found no shared organization in PersonRole for the Zhu Jun-Tang Jie edge, so the Tsinghua colleague claim is unsupported by structured career evidence.
Math Genealogy lists Fei-Fei Li as Andrej Karpathy's doctoral advisor; these PhD-advisor claims point to other people and lack supporting advisor evidence.
OpenAI's launch post supports John Schulman as an early founding member but does not list Dario Amodei as an OpenAI cofounder; this relation conflates later OpenAI overlap with cofounder status.
Britannica identifies DeepMind's founders as Demis Hassabis, Shane Legg and Mustafa Suleyman; Koray Kavukcuoglu is not part of the founder set, so this cofounder relation is a false positive.
VentureBeat identifies Richard Socher as an individual investor in Hugging Face, not as Clément Delangue's advisor or mentor; the available relation type is therefore misleading.
The Transformer paper author list does not include Geoffrey Hinton; these relations used a false 'coauthored Transformer' description and should not be shown as collaborator edges.

Stanford Report's original HAI founder set includes Fei-Fei Li, James Landay, Chris Manning and John Etchemendy; this does not support the claim that Andrew Ng co-founded Stanford HAI with Chris Manning.
The Transformer paper author list does not include Barret Zoph; this relation used a false 'Transformer coauthor' description and should not be shown as a collaborator edge.

Local relation review bucket classifies these as Perplexity colleague_no_shared_org: no shared organization, no overlap, and weak colleague/circle descriptions; product value is lower than risk of showing unsupported relationship edges.
